name: data-validator
description: Validate numeric fields in the input data against min/max bounds. Fails the node if validation does not pass, allowing conditional error edges to handle invalid data.

FIELD.description: Field name in the input data to validate (e.g. 'score', 'count', 'price')

MIN_VALUE.description: Minimum allowed value (inclusive). No minimum check if omitted.

MAX_VALUE.description: Maximum allowed value (inclusive). No maximum check if omitted.

output_shape.field: The name of the validated field (mirrors the FIELD config value)
output_shape.success: true if the value passed validation, false if it failed bounds checks
output_shape.value: The numeric value that was validated (as a JSON number)
output_shape.error: Present only on failure — human-readable description of the bounds violation

setup_instructions.title: Data flow warning — output replaces upstream input
setup_instructions.detail: Data_Validator outputs {"field", "success", "value"} — NOT the full upstream payload. Nodes wired after the validator cannot access the original trigger fields (e.g. order_id, country, items_count) via data["input"]. Use data["__trigger_input__"]["field_name"] to recover original trigger fields in downstream nodes.
